description: Reformat paragraphs from input files (or stdin) to stdout.

option.name: --crown-margin
tip: First and second line of paragraph may have different indentations,
in which case the first line's indentation is preserved, and each subsequent line's indentation matches the second line.

option.name: --exact-prefix
tip: PREFIX must match at the beginning of the line with no preceding whitespace.

option.name: --exact-skip-prefix
tip: PSKIP must match at the beginning of the line with no preceding whitespace.

option.name: --goal
tip: U: -g, --goal <GOAL>
Goal width, default of 93% of WIDTH.
Must be less than or equal to WIDTH.

option.name: --prefix
tip: U: -p, --prefix <PREFIX>
Reformat only lines beginning with PREFIX, reattaching PREFIX to reformatted lines.
Unless -x is specified, leading whitespace will be ignored when matching PREFIX.

option.name: --preserve-headers
tip: Attempt to detect and preserve mail headers in the input.
Be careful when combining this flag with -p.

option.name: --quick
tip: Break lines more quickly at the expense of a potentially more ragged appearance.

option.name: --skip-prefix
tip: U: -P, --skip-prefix <PSKIP>
Do not reformat lines beginning with PSKIP.
Unless -X is specified, leading whitespace will be ignored when matching PSKIP

option.name: --split-only
tip: Split lines only, do not reflow.

option.name: --tab-width
tip: U: -T, --tab-width <TABWIDTH>
Treat tabs as TABWIDTH spaces for determining line length, default 8.
Note that this is used only for calculating line lengths; tabs are preserved in the output.

option.name: --tagged-paragraph
tip: Like -c, except that the first and second line of a paragraph *must* have different indentation or they are treated as separate paragraphs.

option.name: --uniform-spacing
tip: Insert exactly one space between words, and two between sentences.
Sentence breaks in the input are detected as [?!.] followed by two spaces or a newline;
other punctuation is not interpreted as a sentence break.

option.name: --version
tip: Show the version.

option.name: --width
tip: U: -w, --width <WIDTH>
Fill output lines up to a maximum of WIDTH columns, default 75.
This can be specified as a negative number in the first argument.

common_option.name: --help
tip: Show the help.
